step_by_step_reasoning: The transcript describes an ingestion workflow starting from API/Web sources through a gateway and load balancer to ECS. Metadata is stored in RDS while processing queues are managed in SQS. The AI processing layer consists of two distinct EC2 instance classes: CPU instances for pre/post-processing and GPU instances (G5) for inference. Training is decoupled using SageMaker and S3, with Lambda synchronizing the model weights back to the EC2 instances.

graph.name: Spine.ai AI Cataloging Architecture
graph.notes: Processes ~100k images daily using segregated CPU/GPU pipelines and spot instances on SageMaker for cost reduction.

nodes.name: Enterprise Users
nodes.notes: 80+ enterprises in retail, food, fashion, and automobile uploading images and downloading catalogs.

nodes.name: API Gateway
nodes.notes: Handles incoming image uploads from various sources (SDK, API, Web).

nodes.name: Application Load Balancer
nodes.notes: Routes incoming traffic to the appropriate ECS services.

nodes.name: ECS Clusters
nodes.notes: Categorizes images and handles parameters and metadata.

nodes.name: Amazon RDS
nodes.notes: Stores metadata and powers the catalog dashboard.

nodes.name: Amazon SQS
nodes.notes: Task queues mapping to appropriate CPU or GPU processing tasks.

nodes.name: CPU Instances (Pre/Post Processing)
nodes.notes: Performs CPU-intensive tasks like resizing, color correction, and final image construction.

nodes.name: GPU Instances (Inference)
nodes.notes: G5 instances (8x GPUs) running AI model inferences.

nodes.name: Amazon S3
nodes.notes: Stores training datasets, catalog assets, and trained model weights.

nodes.name: Amazon SageMaker
nodes.notes: Runs model training using cost-effective Spot instances.

nodes.name: Weight Syncer Lambda
nodes.notes: Triggered post-benchmarking to synchronize trained model weights from S3 to EC2.

edges.notes: Forwards to appropriate ECS categorization task
Retrieves image payload for model consumption
Powers catalog dashboard for user views and downloads
Pushes new weight models to inference engines
